goal: 优化 hooks status/install 幂等修复、非阻塞与 repo-local/global 提示

summary.title: Harden hook status and install reporting
summary.what: Extended hook installation reporting and status inspection so Cursor, Claude Code, and Codex report repo-local config files, hook coverage counts, repair requirements, dispatcher effectiveness, and restart expectations. Install output now states the repo-local/global boundary and existing sessions restart guidance. Agent install detection now identifies missing, duplicate, or unexpected managed hooks, Codex also checks codex_hooks feature state, and local-dev wrappers fail soft.
summary.why: The hooks UX did not clearly show whether an integration was installed and effective, whether stale or partial config needed repair, whether global settings were touched, or whether a current agent session needed restart. Local-dev hook wrappers could also exit non-zero and block the host agent.
summary.user_goal: 优化 hooks status/install 幂等修复、非阻塞与 repo-local/global 提示

decisions.chose: Add an optional InstallationStatusReporter surface per agent and combine it with dispatcher settings in hooks status.
decisions.rationale: Each agent owns its config schema, while the CLI owns the cross-agent effective/not-effective presentation.

decisions.chose: Treat partial, duplicate, or unexpected managed hooks as needs_repair and make install converge them back to the desired state.
decisions.rationale: This lets status detect stale config and gives users a simple rerun-install recovery path.

decisions.chose: Print repo-local scope and new-session/restart guidance after install and in status.
decisions.rationale: Users need to know Mainline did not modify global agent settings and that existing sessions may not reload hook config immediately.

decisions.chose: Make local-dev wrappers exit 0 when repo lookup or go run fails.
decisions.rationale: Hook failures should not block the host agent session.

rejected.alternative: Keep status as a simple installed boolean
rejected.reason: It cannot distinguish disabled dispatcher, partial stale config, and a fully effective install.

rejected.alternative: Make the CLI parse every agent config directly
rejected.reason: That would centralize agent-specific schemas and duplicate logic already owned by each agent package.

risks: Managed-hook detection is substring-based, so a user-authored hook command containing the same mainline hook subcommand text could be classified as Mainline-managed and removed by uninstall or rewritten by install.

architectural_claims: Agent packages own their native hook config status checks.
The CLI computes effectiveness by combining agent install health with .mainline [hooks] dispatcher settings.
Hooks remain fail-soft and must not block the host agent session.
behavioral_changes: hooks status reports installed/effective/needs repair/dispatcher disabled states with hook counts and repo-local file paths.
hooks install output explicitly says config is repo-local and existing sessions may need a new session or app restart.
Rerunning install repairs partial or stale managed hook config for Cursor, Claude Code, and Codex.
Codex status reports repair needed when codex_hooks is disabled or missing.
local-dev hook wrappers now exit successfully when their command path fails.

data_model_changes.name: hooks.InstallReport
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Added optional scope and restart_required JSON fields.

migration_notes: No manual migration required; rerunning hooks install repairs stale or partial managed config.
